translations:
016 Annaqqa i naqqa d kəbʕi,
			gmaydi i kefayo, kefe manək, məd kaməl u šuġlo ḥaroyo.Zatan
			məd ṭafi, kfayšo  <kfayši>17
			b dukṯayye gnofəl. Ya gəd foyəš hawxa, gšurṭile, bi
			ḥarayto gšurṭile, i naqqa d lo nofəl. Gmaydi ak kefaṯṯe,
			i naqqa d lozəm.

Now
			then when they want, they take away the stone, one of those
			stones, when the first work had been over. After (the fire) had
			already gone out (the stones) remain in their place. (and atuno)
			either falls down or reamins as it is. (In the latter case) They
			take it apart, finally they take it apart, if it does not fall.
			They take away their (own) stones, when it is needed.
			(2018_07_01_01.2 Arkah, Old Houses:16)

Preterit.turoyo: qayəm ġắlabe qḥīr, w naqqa ḥzelan šrīṭ mə-ḥḏoḏe šriṭ u=ḥawy-awo me-ʕal qḏole d-u=qašo w-azzé ʕabər b-gawe d-u=ṣʕuno u=kurrək ḥəlle u=ḥawyano i=ḥayyaṯe gəšriṭo me bayn dam=maye i=kurfaṯe d dawṣo u=ḥolo
translations:
sie
			(die Schlange m) wurde sehr böse, und auf einmal sahen wir, wie
			sie sich auseinanderrollte

‘This snake untwined from the priest’s neck and crawled into
			the sandal’; 47/71
crawled from the water to bite the uncle’ (96:60).

Preterit.turoyo: Šriṭo i fanerayḏi. My sweater was undone (распутился). (İlyas p.c)
